raw_response: Disagree. While both brittleness and poor ductility contribute to the undesirability of beveling amalgam margins, the key factor here specifically relates to how well the material can handle stress without cracking along newly created surfaces. Beveling introduces additional weak points where stress concentrations occur, which is exacerbated by the brittle nature of amalgam leading to increased susceptibility to fracture. Thus, the core issue isn’t just brittleness but also the inherent fragility introduced by creating new interfaces during beveling. My answer remains consistent with Option C: Brittleness.
